channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Wild weather expected Wednesday gets a rare 'high-risk' designation -- the most severe category
item.description: Weather forecasters are using a relatively rare “high-risk” designation to warn that a major tornado outbreak appears likely Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Martin, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Nintendo Switch 2 launches in June with new 'Mario Kart World' game
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Nintendo has announced a June 5 launch date for its latest gaming console, the Switch 2, which will introduce interactive chat and screenshare functions to connect gamers.
item.dc:creator: RYAN KRYSKA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Chocolate bars recalled over possible small stones
item.description: Dutch chocolate maker Tony's Chocoloney has issued a nationwide recall of two of its chocolate products, which may contain small stones.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, KRON4

item.title: James Gunn predicts summer of 'Superman' to the rescue of the box office
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — The 2025 box office has been hit with a deficit. Can the James Gunn-dubbed “summer of ‘Superman’” save it? Warner Bros. showed off a diverse and starry slate of its upcoming films on Tuesday, but the night was carried on the shoulders of Clark Kent.
item.dc:creator: KRYSTA FAURIA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Forecasters warn of deadly floods and strong tornadoes in parts of the Midwest and South
item.description: Potentially deadly flash flooding, high-magnitude tornadoes and baseball-sized hail could hit parts of the Midwest and South on Wednesday as severe thunderstorms blowing eastward become supercharged, forecasters warned.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Abortion, unions and redistricting cases await the Wisconsin Supreme Court after a liberal's win
item.description: Susan Crawford’s Wisconsin Supreme Court election victory over a conservative candidate backed by President Donald Trump and billionaire Elon Musk cements a liberal majority on the state’s top court for at least three more years.
item.dc:creator: Scott Bauer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Josh Brolin, Francis Ford Coppola and Michael Mann pay tribute to Val Kilmer after his death
item.description: Actor Josh Brolin and director Michael Mann are among those paying tribute to Val Kilmer, who died Tuesday at age 65. Here's a look at some of the reactions. “See ya, pal. I’m going to miss you. You were a smart, challenging, brave, uber-creative firecracker. There’s not a lot left of those.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump is set to announce 'reciprocal' tariffs in a risky move that could reshape the economy
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After weeks of White House hype and public anxiety, President Donald Trump is set on Wednesday to announce a barrage of self-described reciprocal tariffs on friend and foe alike. The new tariffs, coming on what Trump has called “Liberation Day,” are a bid to boost U.S.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Gmail planning end-to-end encrypted emails
item.description: Google announced Tuesday that Gmail users will soon be able to send and receive encrypted emails without a third-party provider.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KREX 5

item.title: NFL to use camera technology to measure first downs next season, approves more rule changes
item.description: The NFL announced a multitude of rule changes on Tuesday, including a new way to measure first downs for the 2025 season.
item.dc:creator: Jared Bush, ABC 4

item.title: After layoffs and funding problems, Head Start leaders fear what comes next
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The problems for Head Start began days after President Donald Trump took office. Trump's administration announced it would freeze federal grants — the primary funding for the early education program that serves more than half a million low-income children.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge holds ICE agent in contempt after he detained suspect during a trial
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A judge in Boston is holding a U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement agent in contempt after he detained a suspect while the man was on trial. ICE agent Brian Sullivan detained Wilson Martell-Lebron last week as he was leaving court.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why Tesla may avoid the blow of Trump's auto tariffs
item.description: Elon Musk’s focus on American-made products at Tesla could shield his company from the brunt of President Donald Trump’s new tariffs on foreign vehicles and automobile parts.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 4 key takeaways as Trump’s sweeping HHS layoffs begin
item.description: Thousands of employees across the vast Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) began receiving layoff notices as early as 5 a.m. on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Special election results hint at what’s next for Congress’ power balance and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Detained Tufts student taken from Massachusetts before judge ordered her kept there, government says
item.description: U.S. Justice Department lawyers say a Tufts University doctoral student from Turkey who was detained by immigration authorities had been moved to Vermont by the time a federal judge ordered that she be kept in Massachusetts.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Why no one is challenging Trump's executive order that keeps TikTok running
item.description: President Donald Trump’s executive orders have spurred more than 130 lawsuits, but one related to TikTok barely generates a peep.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Parvini And Mark Sherman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Karen Read jury selection: Dozens in pool already have an opinion on the case
item.description: Dozens of potential jurors told a Massachusetts judge they already had opinions on the Karen Read-John O'Keefe murder case before arriving in court Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Amish buggies targeted in Pennsylvania robbery, attempted robbery
item.description: Some among Pennsylvania's Amish community are concerned that someone may be targeting them, after two buggies were held up at gunpoint, according to State Police.
item.dc:creator: Stan Boney, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge rejects Trump administration’s bid to move Mahmoud Khalil’s legal case to Louisiana
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge has ruled that the legal battle over Mahmoud Khalil’s deportation should continue to play out in New Jersey, rejecting the Trump administration’s bid to transfer the Columbia University protester’s case to Louisiana. In a written decision Tuesday, U.S.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: University of Minnesota student who was detained by ICE sues for immediate release
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A University of Minnesota graduate business student who's being held by Immigration and Customs Enforcement is suing for his immediate release, saying his arrest violated his rights and he's been given little explanation for why he's being held.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Northern Michigan
item.description: The tip of Michigan's mitten struggled through another day without electricity Tuesday as restless residents tried to stay warm while utilities scrambled to restore power in a region waylaid by weekend freezing rain that brought down countless trees and poles.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: San Diego migrant shelter closes doors with numbers plummeting after Trump immigration crackdown: report
item.description: A San Diego migrant shelter is reportedly closing its doors and reverting to its original purpose after the number of asylum seekers dramatically drops.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Social Security Administration probes origin of website portal outage
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Social Security Administration said Tuesday that it is investigating the root cause of website outages that have affected the “my Social Security” portal where recipients access their benefits.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fourth US soldier missing in Lithuania found dead
item.description: “Tragically, three soldiers were found deceased in Lithuania yesterday, and it pains me to confirm that today the fourth soldier was also found deceased,” White House said.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: On the heels of a dry winter, firefighters around the US brace for wildfire risks
item.description: PRESCOTT, Ariz. (AP) — From the southwestern U.S. to Minnesota, Iowa and even parts of New Jersey, it seemed that winter never materialized.
item.dc:creator: TY ONEIL and SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Kidney donor detained by ICE before life-saving transplant can take place
item.description: Immigration rights activists are calling on ICE to release a Venezuelan migrant who was preparing to donate a kidney to his brother, who is in end-stage kidney failure, and has no criminal history.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Spinelli, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 18 people rescued from disabled vessel off San Diego coast
item.description: A 35-foot vessel with 17 migrants and one American aboard began to take water about 50 miles southwest of San Diego, the Coast Guard said.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, NewsNation

item.title: Dave Coulier shares big news about family, cancer fight
item.description: Dave Coulier, who shared he had been diagnosed with Non-Hodgkin lymphoma last year, had some exciting news to share on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cornell student protester facing deportation leaves the US on his 'own terms' after losing faith
item.description: A Cornell University student facing deportation after his visa was revoked because of his campus activism said he decided to leave the United States. Momodou Taal, a citizen of the United Kingdom and Gambia, had asked a federal court to halt his detention.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Rain turns to ice, cuts power in Great Lakes region while Tennessee braces for wild weather
item.description: Freezing rain brought down trees and power lines in Michigan and Wisconsin, cutting electricity for thousands of people Sunday in the upper Great Lakes region, while forecasters said severe weather was on its way to Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Minnesota officials seek answers in case of graduate student detained by ICE
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — Officials in Minnesota are seeking answers in the case of a University of Minnesota graduate student who’s being detained by U.S. immigration authorities for unknown reasons.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Texas reaches 400 measles cases as US deals with outbreaks in 5 states
item.description: At least five states have active measles outbreaks as of Friday, and Texas' is the largest with 400 cases.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4
